title: Double Trestle Play Table
summary: A kid-height play table on two splayed 2x4 trestle ends, with a 1x12 shelf between them and a project-panel top framed by a 1x3 apron.
description:
Each trestle end is built from three 2x4 legs, all cut with matching 15° angles on both ends so the splay is identical from leg to leg — build both trestle ends the same way before joining anything lengthwise. Two 2x2 stretchers run the full length between the two trestle ends, tying them together and giving the shelf something to rest on.

The 1x12 shelf sits between the trestles, trimmed with 1x2 along its edges, and the 1x3 apron frames the underside of the tabletop around the full perimeter, hiding the plywood edge and giving the top something solid to screw into.

The top itself is a single 3/4" project panel, sized to the finished apron footprint — a plain, durable surface that stands up to kid use better than a glued-up board top would on a piece this size.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Both ends cut at 15° off square, long point to short point, ends parallel.
Cut to width of the 1x12 shelf.

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut six trestle legs at 22" from the 2x4. Cut four shelf trim at 11-1/4", two apron sides at 18" and two apron ends at 15" from the 1x2. Cut two shelf at 30-1/2" from the 1x12. Cut four apron frame at 30-1/2" from the 1x3. Cut two stretchers at 65-1/2" from the 2x2. Cut one top at 72" from the 3/4" project panel, 24 in x 72 in. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to two 8 ft 2x4, two 8 ft 1x2, one 6 ft 1x12, two 8 ft 1x3 and two 8 ft 2x2. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw.

steps.title: Build the trestle ends
steps.body: Build two identical trestle end assemblies from the three angled legs each, using pocket holes and glue.

steps.title: Connect the trestles
steps.body: Join the two trestle ends with the two 2x2 stretchers running the full length.

steps.title: Add the shelf
steps.body: Set the 1x12 shelf between the trestles and trim its edges with the 1x2 shelf trim.

steps.title: Build and attach the apron
steps.body: Assemble the 1x3 apron frame and attach it to the underside of the project-panel top around the full perimeter.

steps.title: Attach the top to the base
steps.body: Set the top-and-apron assembly onto the trestle base and fasten.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply a durable primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Double trestle kids play table with shelf
